$curated: State-composition gate for deck-detail (Template B — deck-detail.gen.json has 0 MxCard, so this asserts the keyed node SET per state, not MxCard identity). Only literal-keyed control nodes are gated; chrome (screen/appbar) is excluded (matches dashboard.states.json), and sub-deck / card rows use DYNAMIC keys (deckTile-<id> / cardRow-<id>, covered by the screen's own test) so are not literal-gateable here. `menu` renders whenever the deck node exists (loaded + empty). `add` (FAB) renders only when the node HAS content — the FE hides it in the empty state to match the kit (the empty-add CTA covers add there); so add ∈ loaded, ∉ empty. This gates add against disappearing in loaded AND against leaking into empty, plus the empty-* CTAs (empty-only) — catching the primary THỪA/THIẾU bugs. Cross-checked by test/presentation/features/deck/deck_detail_states_test.dart. COVERAGE GAPS (not gated): error — the DeckDetailNotifier is defensively Result-based (it catches data-source failures and returns a state rather than throwing), so async.when's error branch (_error()/retry) is unreachable from a widget test except by overriding the provider, and deckDetailProvider(deckId).overrideWith(...) does not take effect for this codegen family element here (tried both the throw and Future.error forms, plus closing the db — all fall through to a normal/notFound state); retry therefore stays ungated. deck-delete-confirm (2 keyed dialog buttons via a fragile menu→sheet→dialog tap chain); loading (MxStateView skeleton, no keyed body node); search / no-results (FE routes to /search — ledger search-dock); add-menu (FE add FAB pushes the editor route, no add bottom-sheet — ledger add-word); card-actions / delete-confirm (cards managed in the editor — ledger delete); reset-confirm (v1 has no manual reset — ledger reset); deck-menu (Material bottom-sheet keyed deckAction*, not mx-node — ledger deck-rename/deck-move); move (SimpleDialog, applies immediately — ledger move-apply). deck-detail.slots.json intentionally omitted: the keyed nodes are controls with no bound text slot.
